# Lexicon 480L — Program Default-Parameter Records

Complete dump of the program directory extracted from **ROM1** (64 KiB) of the 480L host firmware set. This is the host-side preset database: the name and default parameter values that get loaded into the HSP signal-processing cards when a program is selected. It is **not** the DSP algorithm code itself (that runs on the HSP cards).

## Record format

ROM1 begins with a 32-bit big-endian pointer table, followed by fixed **144-byte (0x90)** records starting at file offset `0x2000`. Each record is:

| Field | Bytes | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Algorithm ID | 0–1 | 16-bit BE; identifies the underlying algorithm (shared across a bank) |
| Name | 2–13 | 12 chars, space-padded to the LARC display width |
| Default parameters | 14–143 | 65 × 16-bit BE words |

**Caveats for interpretation:** the parameter words are the raw internal representation. The mapping from word position → named parameter (SIZE, RTM, etc.) and the fixed-point scaling are defined by the per-algorithm *page tables* in ROM4 and are **not** applied here — values below are verbatim 16-bit big-endian. Trailing all-zero words are omitted and noted as `(+N×0000)`; interior zeros are preserved (a zero may be a real default).

**Totals:** 205 programs across 21 banks (plus 2 trailing records, see appendix).

## Bank summary

| Bank | Name | Programs | Primary algo ID |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Halls | 10 | 0x02 |
| 2 | Rooms | 10 | 0x02, 0x06 |
| 3 | Wild Spaces | 10 | 0x02, 0x06 |
| 4 | Plates | 5 | 0x06 |
| 5 | Effects | 10 | 0x05 |
| 6 | Twin Delays | 10 | 0x03 |
| 7 | Samplers | 10 | 0x1E, 0x1F, 0x21, 0x22, 0x26, 0x28, 0x2B |
| 8 | Pitch & Doppler | 10 | 0x0B, 0x12 |
| 9 | Mastering | 10 | 0x04, 0x0F, 0x13, 0x32, 0x70, 0x82 |
| 10 | Compressor | 10 | 0x69 |
| 11 | Random Halls | 10 | 0x67 |
